feedback.text: Hello Council, First of all I want to be clear I've been critical of the "I'm European" "movement". We're a collection of individual countries and will never be a super-state like the US. This comes with a big caveat, I am very appreciative of things like the USB-C enforcement and things like free roaming inside the EU. As a new Indie developer I am hoping video games will be included in this "Digital Fairness Act", I've completely lost access to "The Crew" by Ubisoft because they turned off the servers. The bigger companies may claim that they can't afford to have and end-of-life plan, as a retort to this I'd like to offer "RSPS" (or RuneScape Private Servers) as a prime example. These servers were created by industry professionals who wanted to be able to play the game off-line. They were created by interfacing with the game client and COMPLETELY recreating how the game works. The server creation aside from the actual game logic was "Easier" to create for RS because it was written in Java, an older language that's known for not being very secure. So companies wouldn't need to give out any game logic, quite literally they'd only need to release a base of the server that says "Hello World" in the client with basic documentation on how the client handles server requests for assets, animations, graphics/interface calls and sounds. I and people like me would gladly spend hundreds of hours of our own private time to recreate the game logic from the bottom up. If you can show you're only a governing body concerned with consumer rights while also making the containing countries stronger individually and thus also as a union, I might consider greater unification as a concept. I do want to be clear that military unification under an EU-umbrella would be unwise without an immediate change in how you prioritize responsibility. I'm looking forward to seeing how this Act goes and how the EU handles the SKG Initiative, I am glad to see support on the inside for now. Kind regards, Steven Sparringa
